name: Monte Aloia Circular Trail
name_original: Monte Aloia — circular por el Parque Nacional Monte Aloia
description: Hike the easy 3.2-mile Monte Aloia Circular Trail in Parque Natural Monte Aloia, enjoying panoramic views over the Miño River and Tui.
region_intro: Waterfall hiking trails in Monte Aloia are located within Galicia's first Natural Park, near Tui in the province of Pontevedra. The region is characterized by dense forests, diverse natural features, and the Alto de San Xiao peak, reaching an elevation of 2,064 feet (629 meters). Hikers can explore woodlands featuring cork oaks, cypresses, and firs, with trails often leading to panoramic viewpoints over the Miño and Louro valleys. Best waterfall hiking trails in Monte Aloia The most popular waterfall hiking route is Galiñeiro — circular through the Parque Forestal de Zamáns, a 8.4 miles (13.5 km) trail that takes 4 hours 25 minutes to complete. This moderate route features significant elevation gain through varied forest landscapes. Another top favourite among local hikers is Fervenza da Freixa loop from Zamáns, a moderate 4.6 miles (7.4 km) path. This trail offers a shorter option to experience the area's natural beauty, including the Fervenza da Freixa waterfall, and can be completed in about 2 hours 8 minutes. Local hikers also love the Sierra de Galiñeiro loop from O Penedo da Faladoura, a 6.1 miles (9.9 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Galiñeiro mountain range, often completed in about 3 hours 9 minutes. Hiking in Monte Aloia is defined by dense forests, elevated viewpoints, and the presence of the Miño River estuary. The network offers options for different ability levels, with all 4 available routes rated as moderate. The routes in Monte Aloia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 800 reviews. More than 4,700 hikers have used komoot to explore Monte Aloia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many waterfall hiking trails are there in Monte Aloia?
A: Monte Aloia offers 4 dedicated waterfall hiking trails. All of these routes are rated as moderate, providing a good balance of challenge and accessibility for hikers.

Q: Are there any family-friendly waterfall hikes in Monte Aloia?
A: Yes, while all trails are rated moderate, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5328256" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fervenza da Freixa loop from Zamáns&lt;/a&gt; is a shorter option at 4.6 miles (7.4 km) and takes about 2 hours 8 minutes. This makes it a suitable choice for families looking for a less strenuous experience to enjoy the natural beauty, including the Fervenza da Freixa waterfall.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the waterfall trails in Monte Aloia?
A: Monte Aloia is Galicia's first Natural Park, and generally, dogs are welcome on trails as long as they are kept on a leash and owners adhere to park regulations, including cleaning up after their pets. It's always a good idea to check specific signage at trailheads for any particular restrictions.

Q: What are the best viewpoints to see along the waterfall hikes in Monte Aloia?
A: While hiking the waterfall trails, you'll be close to several stunning viewpoints. The park features six integrated viewpoints, with the iconic "Gran Cruz" at the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5125937" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Aloia Summit View&lt;/a&gt; offering expansive vistas of the Miño and Louro valleys, and even the distant Cíes Islands on a clear day. Other notable viewpoints include Mirador do Castelo and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3218559" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Ciao Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or archaeological sites near the waterfall trails in Monte Aloia?
A: Yes, Monte Aloia is rich in history. Hikers can discover the archaeological remains of a Pre-Roman settlement, known as Castro dos Altos Cubos, and explore sections of a cyclopean wall. There's also the hermitage dedicated to Saint Xián and the "San Xián's bed," a strangely shaped rock with local legends. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5860146" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedra do Acordo&lt;/a&gt;, a man-made monument.

Q: What is the best time of year to visit Monte Aloia for waterfall hikes?
A: The best time to visit Monte Aloia for waterfall hikes is typically during the wetter seasons, such as late autumn, winter, and spring. This ensures the waterfalls are at their most impressive. The dense forests also offer pleasant shade during warmer months, but the water flow might be reduced.

Q: Are there any circular waterfall walks in Monte Aloia?
A: Yes, all the waterfall hiking trails in Monte Aloia are circular routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375755565/galineiro-circular-through-the-parque-forestal-de-zamans" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Galiñeiro — circular through the Parque Forestal de Zamáns&lt;/a&gt; is an 8.4-mile (13.5 km) trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31900895" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Galiñeiro loop from O Penedo da Faladoura&lt;/a&gt; is a 6.1-mile (9.9 km) loop.

Q: How can I get to Monte Aloia's waterfall trails using public transport?
A: Monte Aloia Natural Park is located near Tui in the province of Pontevedra. While direct public transport to the trailheads within the park might be limited, you can typically reach Tui by bus. From Tui, you would likely need to arrange for a taxi or a local ride-sharing service to get to the park's entrance or specific starting points for the waterfall trails.

Q: Where can I park when visiting the waterfall trails in Monte Aloia?
A: Monte Aloia Natural Park generally offers designated parking areas for visitors. The Engineer Ramón Areses' Forest House, which serves as an interpretation center, is a common starting point and usually has parking facilities. Look for marked parking zones near the main entrances or popular trailheads within the park.

Q: What do other hikers say about the waterfall trails in Monte Aloia?
A: The waterfall trails in Monte Aloia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 800 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse forest landscapes, the stunning panoramic views from elevated points, and the well-maintained paths that lead to natural features like the Fervenza da Freixa waterfall.

Q: Are there any regulations or permits required for hiking in Monte Aloia Natural Park?
A: As Monte Aloia is Galicia's first Natural Park, there are regulations in place to protect its environment. Generally, no specific permits are required for day hiking on marked trails. However, visitors are expected to respect the natural surroundings, stay on designated paths, refrain from littering, and follow any rules regarding fires or wildlife. Always check the official park website for the most current regulations.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the waterfall hikes in Monte Aloia?
A: The waterfall hikes in Monte Aloia feature varied terrain, characterized by dense forests with cork oaks, cypresses, holly, firs, and Lebanon cedars. You can expect significant elevation gain on some routes, leading to elevated viewpoints. Trails often include picturesque walkways and wooden bridges, especially along routes like the Muíños do Tripes Trail, and ancient stone waterways on the Rego da Pedra Route.

Q: Are there any places to eat or stay near the waterfall trails in Monte Aloia?
A: While the park itself is primarily natural, the nearby town of Tui offers various amenities including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. Within Monte Aloia, the Engineer Ramón Areses' Forest House serves as an interpretation center and may have limited facilities, but for a wider selection of services, Tui is your best bet.
tips: Mount paradise for fans of both mtb and enduro and trekking, spectacular tracks and trails, viewpoints,....
If you decide to explore this route, you will be surrounded by nature and stunning views. Enjoy your hike! 🏞️🚶‍♂️🥾🚵🌞

Mount Aloia is a beautiful place full of nature, this natural park is located on the southern limit of the Serra do Galiñeiro. One of its main attractions are the panoramic views of the valleys of the Miño and Louro rivers. The highest point is Alto de San Xiao (631 m), which is the most visited place in this enclave. Mount Aloia was declared the first Natural Park of Galicia on October 4, 1978. Although it is the smallest of the natural parks in the community, it has an extensive network of paths, 6 viewpoints and picnic areas to enjoy nature alongside the “wet stripe” (border) with Portugal.
Near Mount Aloia, there is a Via Crucis that culminates at the Cruz viewpoint, which dates back to 1910. This viewpoint offers impressive views and is a must-see place for cycle tourists and lovers of hiking and nature.
If you have the opportunity to visit Mount Aloia, I recommend exploring these places and enjoying the natural beauty it offers. I hope you have a wonderful experience! 😊

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Monte Aloia Circular Trail?
faqs.answer: The Monte Aloia Natural Park has designated parking areas, typically near the visitor center or main access points. You can usually find parking close to the start of the circular route, making it convenient to begin your hike.

faqs.question: Is the Monte Aloia Circular Trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this specific circular trail is generally considered an easy hike, making it suitable for all fitness levels, including beginners and families. Its paths are easily accessible, and the overall elevation gain is moderate.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features mostly shaded paths winding through forests. Many segments are well-paved and easy to navigate. The park's landscape is characterized by its granite composition and rich vegetation, offering a pleasant walking surface.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers impressive natural beauty and panoramic vistas. You'll find several viewpoints with stunning views over the Miño River, the surrounding valley, the city of Tui, and even into neighboring Portugal. Notable highlights include Monte Aloia, San Ciao Viewpoint, and the View from Alto de San Xiao, Monte Aloia. The park also features historical remains, the Chapel of San Xián, and a botanical trail.

faqs.question: Is the Monte Aloia Circular Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Monte Aloia Natural Park generally welcomes dogs, it's always best to keep them on a leash to protect the local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all visitors. Please remember to clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn are particularly beautiful. In these seasons, the rich vegetation offers a 'symphony of colour' with various flowering plants and changing leaves. Summer provides pleasant shade, while winter can offer crisp views, though some paths might be damp.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Monte Aloia Natural Park?
faqs.answer: As the first natural park declared in Galicia, Monte Aloia Natural Park is generally open to the public without specific entrance fees or permits for day visits to its trails. However, it's always a good idea to check the official park website for any updated regulations or special event requirements before your visit.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Monte Aloia Circular Trail is situated within a network of paths. This route specifically covers significant portions of other official trails, including [CIMA PO11] Monte Aloia * Rebordáns / San Fins, [CIMA PO11] Monte Aloia * Tui, and [CIMA PO11] Monte Aloia * Prado. It also briefly intersects with the Galiñeiro-Aloia and Sendeiro da Cabaciña trails, and a small section of the Sendeiro Botánico.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife or plants might I see?
faqs.answer: The park is known for its rich and diverse vegetation, including indigenous forests with deciduous species, alongside exotic trees like cypresses, firs, Lebanese cedars, cork oaks, and holly. While specific wildlife sightings can vary, the park is home to various birds and small mammals. A dedicated botanical trail with explanatory panels can help you identify many plant species.

faqs.question: Are there facilities like restrooms or water sources along the trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is a natural path, the Monte Aloia Natural Park typically has facilities such as restrooms and a visitor center (Engineer Rafael Areses' Forest House) near the main access points. It's advisable to carry your own water, especially on warmer days, as direct water sources on the trail might be limited.

faqs.question: What historical or cultural significance does Monte Aloia hold?
faqs.answer: Monte Aloia is steeped in history, being the first natural park in Galicia. It contains prehistoric and Roman remains, including the Castro Alto dos Cubos (an ancient hilltop fort) and petroglyphs. You can also find a mysterious cyclopean wall at the summit and the Romanesque Chapel of San Xián, which is a center of local traditions. The area is also touched by the Camino Portugués.
